data.overview: M&E projects typically involve 20–200+ drawings. Services appear across multiple sheets. Cross-referencing ensures nothing is double-counted and nothing is missed. This is where most estimation errors occur.

drawing_hierarchy.rule: When the same item appears on multiple drawing types, use this hierarchy to determine which source to measure from:

priority_order.use_for: Definitive count, specification, and sizing. ALWAYS use schedule count over drawing count.
Linear measurement (meterage), routing, spatial layout. PRIMARY source for quantities.
Clash-resolved routing. Use ONLY if more detailed than GA. Flag as preliminary if status is 'For Coordination'.
Terminal device positions (diffusers, grilles, luminaires). NOT for duct/pipe routing above ceiling.
Vertical measurements, riser heights, equipment clearances. Supplements plan measurements.
System connectivity, equipment list, valve locations. NEVER for linear measurements.
Installation method, connection details. NOT for quantities unless explicitly stating a dimension.

schedule_validation.rule: Cross-check every equipment schedule against the drawings. Discrepancies are common and must be flagged.

checks.common_discrepancies: Schedule lists 8 FCUs but only 7 shown on drawing (one may be on another floor's drawing, or schedule is wrong)
checks.resolution: Flag discrepancy. Use schedule count. Note which items cannot be located on drawings.

checks.common_discrepancies: Valve schedule shows isolation valves not drawn on GA. P&ID shows valves schematically but not in correct physical location.
checks.resolution: Use valve schedule for count. Use GA for location. If valve appears on P&ID but not GA, flag as [SCHEDULE ITEM — NOT LOCATED ON GA].

checks.common_discrepancies: Schedule room-by-room count differs from RCP symbol count
checks.resolution: Use schedule count (designer's intent). Verify RCP shows same distribution pattern.

checks.common_discrepancies: Cable schedule lists circuits not shown on containment drawings, or cable sizes differ from SLD
checks.resolution: Cable schedule is authoritative for cable type and size. Containment drawings for routing.

checks.common_discrepancies: Schedule includes terminals in rooms not shown on ductwork GA (may be on another drawing)
checks.resolution: Use schedule for count and specification. Flag terminals that cannot be located on any drawing.

multi_sheet_reconciliation.rule: Services crossing between drawing sheets must be measured once only. Use match lines and drawing references to track continuity.
process: 1. BEFORE measuring, list all drawings in the set. Note which areas each covers (floor, zone, gridlines).
2. IDENTIFY match lines — dashed lines at drawing edges showing where the adjacent sheet picks up.
3. CHECK for overlap — some drawings overlap by one or two gridlines. Measure to the match line on one sheet only.
4. WHERE a service crosses from one sheet to another, measure to the drawing edge and note: 'Continues on Dwg M-202'.
5. WHEN measuring the adjacent sheet, start from its edge. Do NOT double-count the overlap zone.
6. RISER connections between floors: measure the riser once (typically from the lowest drawing that shows it). Note which floors the riser serves.
double_count_risks: Same corridor shown on two adjacent floor plan sheets — measure from one sheet only
Plant room straddling two drawing sheets — measure equipment from the most detailed sheet
Riser shown on every floor plan — measure riser once from section drawing or lowest floor
Equipment on both GA and equipment layout drawing — count from schedule, verify on GA
Service in revision cloud on both old and new revision — use latest revision only

floor_to_floor_reconciliation.rule: Track services that cross between floors to ensure complete measurement without duplication.
process: Build a RISER REGISTER: for each riser, record service type, sizes, which floors it serves, and which drawing it's measured from.
HORIZONTAL distribution on each floor is measured from that floor's GA.
VERTICAL risers are measured separately: number of floors × floor-to-floor height.
At each floor landing, count: isolation valves, branch connections, fire stopping. These are per-floor items.
Where a service drops from ceiling to equipment (e.g., pipe from tray to FCU), add the vertical drop per unit.

revision_management.rule: Only measure from the LATEST revision of each drawing. Superseded revisions can cause significant errors.
process: 1. Check drawing revision letter/number in title block before measuring.
2. If drawing set contains multiple revisions of the same drawing, use the highest revision only.
3. Note revision clouds — these indicate what changed. Items inside clouds are new/modified scope.
4. Flag items in revision clouds: [REVISED AREA — verify quantities are current].
5. If a revision cloud removes items (deletion cloud), ensure those items are NOT in your take-off.
